page: The UpCloud PREMIUM-8xCPU-64GB is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 8 shared vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core. Storage is bundled with a 300 GB local SSD, and virtualization is managed via the KVM hypervisor. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AES cryptography, and SHA extensions.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring 3567.67 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 37.59 GB/sec. Multi-core performance falls into the average range, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 22249.25 and a PassMark CPU score of 16952.1. Memory read bandwidth is average at 1524.49 GB/sec for cached small blocks, but large-block memory write bandwidth is weak at 19.66 GB/sec, which is reflected in a lower PassMark memory score of 1559.63.

This server is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and web serving, as demonstrated by its average database operations score of 6117.66 and static web serving throughput of 11.68 GB/sec. However, the shared CPU allocation and weak large-block memory write speeds present tradeoffs for write-heavy applications or intensive multi-threaded tasks. It is also capable of handling light LLM inference, achieving 233.79 tokens/sec on text generation for a small 135M model, though performance drops significantly on larger models.
description: The UpCloud PREMIUM-8xCPU-64GB is an x86_64 virtual server configured with 8 shared AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 64.0 GB of RAM, and a 300 GB local SSD. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 3567.67 and high single-threaded compression speeds. However, its multi-core capabilities and memory write bandwidth are average to weak, with a large-block write speed of 19.66 GB/sec. With a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU resources. It is well-suited for general-purpose applications, database operations, and static web serving.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-8xCPU-64GB is an x86_64 KVM server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 300 GB SSD, offering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-8xCPU-64GB is an x86_64 KVM server featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 300 GB SSD for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual server featuring a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, top-tier single-core performance, and 300 GB of local SSD storage.

300 GB local SSD storage and KVM hypervisor
Top-tier single-core performance and single-threaded compression
Average multi-core performance and database operations throughput
Suited for memory-intensive workloads, web serving, and databases
